industry_primer: The yoga industry in India is highly competitive, with numerous studios and instructors offering a range of classes and styles. Key economics include high demand for weekday evening and weekend morning classes, with average revenue per user (ARPU) ranging from ₹800 to ₹1,200 per month. Regulatory backdrop is relatively minimal, with some studios obtaining certifications from organizations like the Yoga Alliance. Typical customer behavior includes a high propensity to attend classes regularly, with an average customer lifetime value (CLTV) of ₹10,000 to ₹15,000.

real_world_numbers.metric: Yoga studio market size in India
Number of yoga studios in India
Average revenue per user (ARPU) for yoga studios
Customer lifetime value (CLTV) for yoga studios

expected_math.step: Calculate the total area of the studio
Calculate the area of a single yoga mat
Calculate the maximum number of mats that can fit in the studio
expected_math.formula: total area ÷ (mat area + minimum distance between mats)

expected_math.step: Calculate the wasted space in the studio
expected_math.formula: total area - (number of mats × mat area)

common_mistakes: Not accounting for the minimum distance between mats and walls
Not considering the instructor's space requirements
Overestimating the number of mats that can fit in the studio
Not calculating the wasted space in the studio
Not researching industry benchmarks for yoga studio sizes and class sizes

anticipated_questions.q: What is the average size of a yoga studio in India?
How many yoga studios are there in India?
What is the typical revenue model for yoga studios?
How do yoga studios typically market themselves?
anticipated_questions.a: Social media, word-of-mouth, and local advertising

anticipated_questions.q: What are the key factors that affect the demand for yoga classes?
anticipated_questions.a: Time of day, day of the week, and instructor reputation

anticipated_questions.q: How can yoga studios optimize their class schedules to maximize revenue?
anticipated_questions.a: Offering classes during peak hours, using data analytics to optimize scheduling

anticipated_questions.q: What are the common pitfalls that yoga studios face when trying to increase class size?
anticipated_questions.a: Overcrowding, inadequate instructor training, and poor customer service

anticipated_questions.q: How can yoga studios use data analytics to improve their operations?
anticipated_questions.a: Tracking attendance, revenue, and customer feedback to inform business decisions

framework_hints: Consider the spatial constraints of the studio and the minimum distance between mats
Think about the instructor's role in the class and how it affects the number of mats that can fit
Research industry benchmarks for yoga studio sizes and class sizes to inform your calculations
case_type_notes: This operations case requires the candidate to think spatially and consider the constraints of the studio, while also researching industry benchmarks and best practices to inform their calculations.
